title: Discrete-time hazard model over laps-since-stint-start
summary: This target is naturally a conditional event probability: stop on the next lap given survival so far.
rationale: This target is naturally a conditional event probability: stop on the next lap given survival so far. Discrete-time survival literature shows tree or neural classifiers on person-period data can match or beat standard survival baselines and gives a cleaner way to model baseline hazard.
implementation_hint: Build a hazard-style table where each lap is an at-risk interval within the current stint, add spline/bin features for laps-in-stint and laps-remaining, and predict conditional stop probability next lap. Enforce fold-safe construction and compare against the current rowwise classifier on held-out Race_Year folds.
expected_effect: Should capture the non-linear stop hazard curve within a stint and reduce ad hoc threshold features.
risk: If expanded carelessly, person-period framing can overweight long non-event stints and worsen imbalance; weighting and grouped CV matter.
